commit.message:
Movement checks (#170)

* Replace wall collision penalty with proactive movement masking

- Add Room.can_link_move_from() for pixel-level movement checks using
  NES ObjGridOffset (tile checks only fire at grid boundaries)
- Add ZeldaGame.can_link_move() with locked-door-with-key override
- Add link_grid_offset to data.json (NES RAM 94)
- Remove WALL_COLLISION_PENALTY and _did_link_run_into_wall from critics
- Replace reactive _handle_wall_bump/move_mask in action_space with
  proactive per-direction can_link_move() calls in the mask builder
- Remove prevent_wall_bumping parameter from ZeldaActionSpace
- Replace stuck_count>=8 heuristic in frame_skip_wrapper with
  deterministic can_link_move_from() check at movement start
- Update tests: wall collision test → movement masking test,
  adjust stalfos injury timing for fewer wasted frames

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix movement masking: always check tiles regardless of grid_offset

ObjGridOffset is axis-agnostic — a nonzero offset from vertical movement
was incorrectly allowing horizontal moves into walls. The tile check is
conservative and never produces false positives, so always apply it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Always build Room from current RAM tiles to fix stale door corridors

ZeldaGame.room now always calls Room.create() with current_tiles instead
of preferring the cache via Room.get(). Room.create() short-circuits with
torch.equal() when tiles haven't changed, so the common case is cheap.

This fixes stale corridor tiles when doors open (key used or enemies
cleared) — the cached Room previously retained the locked-door tile
layout, causing movement masking to block valid corridor movement.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Allow movement into cave entry tiles for proactive masking

Cave top tile (0xF3) is above OW_FIRST_UNWALKABLE but the NES triggers
a cave transition via CheckPassiveTileObjects (Z_07.asm:2957-2962) when
Link walks into it.  Treat it as walkable for movement masking so the
agent can enter caves.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Restrict locked-door-with-key override to doorway corridor

The NES CheckDoorway (Z_05.asm:3755) only fires when Link's
perpendicular coordinate matches the doorway: X=$78 for N/S doors,
Y=$8D for E/W doors.  Without this position check, any room with a
locked door would allow movement toward the door from anywhere in the
room, even when blocked by walls/blocks (e.g. debug_1_45.state).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix empty wavefront when exit seed is blocked by obstacles

The wavefront used a single center exit tile offset off-screen as its
BFS seed.  If obstacles blocked expansion from that one point, the
wavefront never reached the rest of the room (e.g. debug_0_68.state).

Two fixes:
- Use ALL exit tiles as off-screen seeds so the BFS can expand from
  every exit point, not just the center.
- Skip can_move checks when expanding from off-screen seeds since they
  are virtual positions with no real NES tiles.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update scenarios

* Fix pixel-alignment bug in can_link_move_from using exact NES hotspots

can_link_move_from was delegating to can_move which used approximate tile-
coordinate offsets (tr+1 for feet). The NES computes hotspot pixel positions
directly: base_Y = ObjY + $0B, then subtracts $40 for the status bar before
dividing by 8 to get the tile row.

The old formula (py - 56) // 8 + 1 disagreed with the NES formula
(py - 53) // 8 for 62% of Y positions (when (py-64) % 8 < 5), checking
one row too low. This caused false positives — allowing movement the NES
would block.

Rewrote can_link_move_from to compute exact NES hotspot positions:
- Left:  col=(px-8)//8,   row=(py-53)//8
- Right: col=(px+16)//8,  row=(py-53)//8
- Up:    col=px//8,        row=(py-61)//8,  checks col and col+1
- Down:  col=px//8,        row=(py-45)//8,  checks col and col+1

can_move (tile-space) left unchanged for wavefront BFS which is internally
consistent with its approximate offsets.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix security vulnerabilities in auth endpoints (closes #13) (#15)

Security Fixes:
- Add open redirect prevention: Validate returnUrl is local path before redirecting
- Change logout from GET to POST: Prevents CSRF logout attacks
- Update LoginDisplay.razor: Use form with POST method and AntiforgeryToken

Implementation Details:
- Added IsLocalUrl() helper to validate URLs start with / but not //
- Rejects http://, https://, and protocol-relative URLs
- Logout endpoint now requires POST method
- Antiforgery validation via UseAntiforgery() middleware

Testing:
- Added Web.Tests project with integration tests
- Tests verify URL validation logic for open redirect protection
- Tests verify GET logout returns 404 (endpoint removed)
- Tests verify POST logout requires authentication
- All 12 tests passing

References:
- OWASP Open Redirect: https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Unvalidated_Redirects_and_Forwards_Cheat_Sheet.html

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: fix auth by writing auth.json instead of broken --token flag

commit.message:
fix: allow optional percentage in summarize schemas (#2)

* fix: allow optional percentage in summarize schemas

SummarizeRequest and SummarizeAgainRequest had `percentage: int` with
`ge=30, le=80`, which rejected `0` or `null` from the frontend with a
422 Unprocessable Entity.

Changed both to `int | None` with `ge=1, le=100` so callers can omit
or null-out the field; `_get_percentage()` already handles the
default fallback to a random 30-80 value.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: widen percentage range to 1-100 across models and simplify fallback

- Text model: percentage ge=30,le=80 → ge=1,le=100

  default of 50%; respect user's explicit choice via schema validation
- Remove unused `random` import

* ci: add GitHub Actions workflow to auto-deploy to FastAPI Cloud

auth token from the FASTAPI_CLOUD_AUTH GitHub secret, then runs
`fastapi deploy . --no-wait` which uses the app_id from .fastapicloud/cloud.json.

Setup required:
1. Copy the contents of ~/.fastapi/auth.json on your local machine
2. Add it as a GitHub secret named FASTAPI_CLOUD_AUTH in the repo settings

fix(config): resolve CORS_ORIGINS SettingsError on startup

pydantic-settings v2 calls json.loads() on list[str] fields before
field_validator runs, causing a SettingsError if the env var is not
valid JSON (e.g. plain URL or comma-separated string in a dashboard).

- Change CORS_ORIGINS type from list[str] to str to bypass pydantic-
  settings' complex-type JSON pre-parsing
- Replace field_validator with a @computed_field property cors_origins
  that accepts JSON arrays, comma-separated values, or a single URL

- Update .env.example to document both accepted formats

commit.message:
FIX: Resolve camera spinning by properly disabling CameraFollow in FPS mode

ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS:
- CameraFollow is a third-person camera controller that orbits around the player
- PlayerController implements FPS camera with direct mouse look (rotates player)
- When both components are enabled, they conflict over rotation control
- Result: Infinite spinning as each tries to override the other

SOLUTION:
- Added DisableCameraFollowConflicts() method to explicitly disable CameraFollow
- This ensures clean FPS mode without competing camera systems
- Updated documentation to clarify FPS-only architecture
- Code is now self-documenting about the architectural choice

TESTING:
- Build: 0 errors (pre-existing warnings only)
- Verified: PlayerController.cs compiles cleanly
- DisableCameraFollowConflicts() safely handles missing components

This is a proper fix (not just disabling a symptom) by addressing the architectural
conflict between third-person and first-person camera systems.

ENHANCED: Strengthen maze shortcut detection & prevention

CRITICAL BUG #3 - Maze Wall Generation (ENHANCED 2026-03-12)
- Previous: Added basic validation warnings
- Now: Comprehensive shortcut detection + enhanced path generation
- Impact: Eliminates potential unintended shortcuts between spawn/exit

VERIFICATION ENHANCEMENTS:
=============================

1. VerifyMazeIntegrity() - Now includes path length analysis
   * Calculates actual path length using BFS
   * Compares to direct Manhattan distance
   * Warns if path ratio < 1.5x (indicates shortcuts)
   * Critical error if path shorter than direct distance
   * Logs detailed analysis: passages%, spawn/exit marks, path ratio

2. FindActualPathLength() - New BFS pathfinding validator
   * Traces actual walkable path from spawn to exit
   * Returns cell count of shortest playable path
   * Used to detect unintended shortcuts
   * Returns worst-case estimate if path blocked

SHORTCUT PREVENTION ENHANCEMENTS:
==================================

3. CarveIndirectPath() - Strengthened waypoint generation
   * Increased waypoints: 3-4 (was 2-4)
   * Added minimum distance between waypoints (grid/4)
   * Waypoints must be far apart to force longer paths
   * Increased wall penalty: 10x (was 5x)
   * Better logging with segment distances

VALIDATION LOGIC:
=================
- Path ratio < 1.5x: WARNING (suspicious shortcut)
- Path shorter than direct: CRITICAL (shortcut confirmed)
- Passages 15-60%: OK (healthy density)
- Passages <15%: WARNING (too dense)
- Passages >60%: WARNING (too many shortcuts)

Example outputs:
  'Path analysis: Direct distance=30 cells, Actual path=50 cells, Ratio=1.67x'
  'Path suspiciously short (1.2x direct distance). Possible shortcut carved by A*!'
  'Path is SHORTER than direct distance! Unintended shortcut detected!'

==================

EXPECTED RESULTS:

- Spawn/exit properly isolated with walls between

- Console logs detailed integrity analysis per generation
